title: Automated Scanners: OpenVAS & Nikto 🤖
description: Run industry-standard vulnerability scanners against your lab.

slides.title: Why Automate? 🦾
slides.content:
# Speed and Coverage

Manually checking every port for every CVE is impossible. **Vulnerability Scanners** automate this process by sending thousands of checks against a target.

**Two Types of Scanners:**
- **Network Scanners**: Scan entire IP ranges for OS and service vulnerabilities. (e.g., **OpenVAS**, **Nessus**, **Qualys**).
- **Web Application Scanners**: Focus specifically on web vulnerabilities like SQLi and XSS. (e.g., **Nikto**, **Burp Suite Scanner**, **Acunetix**).

**Analogy**: If manual checking is a detective with a magnifying glass, an automated scanner is a team of 100 detectives with checklists.

slides.title: OpenVAS (Greenbone) 🟢
slides.content:
# The Open Source Powerhouse

**OpenVAS** (Open Vulnerability Assessment System) is the open-source fork of Nessus. It is the go-to free network vulnerability scanner.

**Components:**
- **Greenbone Security Assistant (GSA)**: Web interface.
- **OpenVAS Scanner**: The engine that runs the tests.
- **Feed**: Daily updates of vulnerability tests (NVTs).

**Installation on Kali:**
```bash
sudo apt install openvas
sudo gvm-setup
```
*Note: OpenVAS is resource-heavy. It may be slow on a VM with limited RAM. For this course, we focus on the lighter **Nikto** for lab practice, but you should know OpenVAS exists.*

slides.title: OpenVAS Alternative: Nessus 🚀
slides.content:
## The Industry Standard

**Nessus** by Tenable is the most widely used commercial vulnerability scanner. It offers a **free 'Home' license** for up to 16 IPs.

**Why mention it?** If you pursue a career in pentesting, you **will** use Nessus. The interface and logic are nearly identical to OpenVAS. Learning one teaches you 90% of the other.

slides.title: Nikto: Web Server Scanner 🕸️
slides.content:
# Lightweight and Loud

**Nikto** is an open-source web server scanner that performs comprehensive tests against web servers for multiple items, including:
- Over 6700 potentially dangerous files/programs.
- Outdated versions of over 1250 servers.
- Version specific problems on over 270 servers.
- Server configuration items (e.g., Indexes enabled, TRACE methods).

**Command Syntax:**
```bash
nikto -h http://192.168.56.101
```

**Pros**: Fast, easy, great for finding low-hanging fruit like `/phpinfo.php` or `/admin/`.
**Cons**: **Extremely noisy**. It sends thousands of requests and will light up any IDS.

slides.title: Nikto in Action 💻
slides.content: Run Nikto against your Metasploitable VM's web server.
slides.codeSnippet:
# Basic scan
nikto -h http://192.168.56.101

# Scan on different port
nikto -h http://192.168.56.101:8080

# Save output to file
nikto -h http://192.168.56.101 -o nikto_results.txt

# Tune specific tests (e.g., file uploads)
nikto -h http://192.168.56.101 -Tuning 5

quizData.question: Which tool is specifically designed for comprehensive web server scanning, checking for outdated software and dangerous files?
quizData.explanation: Nikto is a dedicated web server scanner. OpenVAS scans the whole network stack.

slides.title: Interpreting Scanner Results 📋
slides.content:
# Separating Signal from Noise

Automated scanners produce **a lot** of findings. Not all are accurate.

**1. False Positives**
The scanner says a vulnerability exists, but it doesn't (e.g., banner misidentification). **Always verify manually.**

**2. Informational**
Not a vulnerability, just a fact (e.g., 'Allowed HTTP Methods: GET, POST'). Useful for context.

**3. Low / Medium / High**
Categorized by CVSS.

**Hacker Workflow**: Look at the **High/Critical** findings first. Try to exploit them. If you can't, investigate why (False Positive?).

slides.title: Scanning Etiquette ⚠️
slides.content:
## Never Scan Production Without Permission

Vulnerability scanners are **intrusive**. They can:
- Crash fragile services (e.g., old printers, IoT devices).
- Fill up log files (causing disk space issues).
- Trigger alarms and panic the SOC.

**In a real test**, coordinate with the client. Often, scanning is done **outside business hours**.
